extraction.one_line_summary: GROENEWEGEN Dylan won the bunch sprint after DILLIER Silvan raided intermediate sprints twice for bonus seconds, positioning himself 2nd overall
tactical_patterns: DILLIER Silvan contested both intermediate sprints for bonus seconds despite being sprinter, placing 2nd GC via time bonuses
Quick-Step launched late attack inside 10km not for stage win but to deliver JAKOBSEN Fabio armchair sprint position
Lotto-Soudal never let early break exceed 1min gap despite short 154km stage, controlling for GROENEWEGEN sprint

rider_signals.DILLIER Silvan: positive: aggressively hunted bonus seconds at intermediates as GC strategy, finished 10th overall previous year using same tactic
rider_signals.GROENEWEGEN Dylan: positive: 14th win of season, delivered by Lotto-Soudal tight pace control throughout short high-speed stage
rider_signals.JAKOBSEN Fabio: positive: Quick-Step tactical sacrifice late to position him, five wins in debut season suggests emerging sprint threat
rider_signals.ACKERMANN Pascal: neutral: visible in finale but beaten by GROENEWEGEN, Bora-Hansgrohe had HALLER Marco in early break taking first sprint
rider_signals.HALLER Marco: positive: returned from 6-month broken kneecap layoff to contest early break and win first intermediate sprint
